svg.text: 4 ESP32 nodes, 3 I2C sensors, 4 MOSFET fan channels, 2 LED channels, 1 camera, 2 smart plugs
SporePrint Server | Mosquitto MQTT :1883 | Web UI :3001
ESP32-S3 DevKit | USB-C 5V | 3 I2C sensors on shared bus
ESP32-S3 DevKit | USB-C 5V | 4 MOSFET channels driving 12V fans
CH0: FAE Fan — Full MOSFET Circuit Detail
Each channel is identical: GPIO -> 100R -> IRLZ44N Gate | 10K pull-down Gate-to-GND | Drain -> Load(-) -> Load(+) -> +12V | 1N4007 flyback across load | Source -> GND
ESP32-S3 DevKit | USB-C 5V | 2 MOSFET channels, 10-bit PWM dimming
Same MOSFET circuit as relay node (100R gate, 10K pull-down, 1N4007 flyback). Shares 12V PSU with relay node.
No GPIO wiring. Captures every 15min via WiFi.
Powers all fans (relay node) + LED strips (lighting node) via MOSFET drain circuits
Same MOSFET circuit as relay, with LED strip
instead of fan. CH0: White (GPIO 25), CH1: Blue (GPIO 26).
10-bit PWM for smooth dimming. Shares 12V PSU.
USB-C power only. No wiring. Connects via WiFi/MQTT.
Plug in humidifier + heater/cooler. WiFi only. No wiring.
Tip: All I2C sensors share the same 4-wire bus (daisy-chain OK). Each MOSFET channel needs its own GPIO but shares 12V and GND rails.
Pi, camera, and smart plugs communicate wirelessly only -- no physical wires to them.
